behavior: Conspicuous, intelligent ground-foraging omnivore; walks and hops with the tail often raised, caches food, gives harsh alarm chatter, and commonly travels in pairs, family parties, or larger roosting groups. Flight is usually low, with rapid wingbeats alternating with glides.
field_marks: Bold black-and-white plumage with a white belly and scapular patches
Dark wings and tail show iridescent blue-green or violet gloss
Very long, graduated tail makes up more than half the adult's length
Short, rounded wings reveal large white primary patches in flight
Stout, straight black bill and dark-brown eye
habitat: Open and semiopen landscapes with scattered trees, including farmland, woodland edges, parks, gardens, and other developed areas. Generally avoids dense forest, extensive wetlands, and treeless country.
